* Merge devops changes into copilot/fix-9115570-1194939430-27f5cb02-5202-4511-a141-de5e97b6f5b1

* fix: use existing saved pipeline when creating parent issues from chat (#1170)

* fix: use existing saved pipeline when creating parent issues from chat

When the chat agent creates a GitHub Parent Issue, the system now
correctly uses the user's currently selected/assigned Agent Pipeline
instead of falling back to system defaults or requiring an explicit
@mention.

Previously, three gaps caused pipeline selection to be lost:

1. confirm_recommendation (workflow.py) had no fallback — unlike
   confirm_proposal which called resolve_project_pipeline_mappings()
   when no pipeline_id was explicitly provided, confirm_recommendation
   used _apply_selected_pipeline() which was a no-op when
   selected_pipeline_id was None. This meant ISSUE_CREATE actions
   from the chat agent always used system defaults instead of the
   project's assigned pipeline.

   Fix: Replaced the _apply_selected_pipeline call with the same
   3-tier resolution logic used by confirm_proposal:
   - Tier 1: Explicit pipeline_id from @mention
   - Tier 2: Project-level pipeline assignment
   - Tier 3: User-specific / system default mappings
   Also correctly propagates stage_execution_modes and group_mappings
   from the resolved pipeline to the workflow config.

2. Streaming endpoint (chat.py) dropped pipeline_id — the
   send_message_stream SSE endpoint did not forward pipeline_id or
   file_urls to ChatAgentService.run_stream(), so the agent's session
   state lacked pipeline context during streaming conversations.

   Fix: Added pipeline_id and file_urls kwargs to the run_stream()
   call in the streaming endpoint.

3. Frontend didn't auto-include the project's assigned pipeline —
   ChatInterface.tsx only sent a pipeline_id when the user explicitly
   typed @ and selected a pipeline from the autocomplete dropdown.
   Normal chat messages had no pipeline_id attached.

   Fix: Added useSelectedPipeline hook to ChatInterface which reads
   the project's assigned pipeline. The @mentioned pipeline takes
   priority; the project assignment is used as a fallback. This
   ensures every chat-created issue routes through the saved pipeline
   without requiring the user to @mention it each time.

Files changed:
- solune/backend/src/api/workflow.py: Full pipeline resolution in
  confirm_recommendation with 3-tier fallback
- solune/backend/src/api/chat.py: Pass pipeline_id/file_urls to
  streaming agent
- solune/backend/src/services/chat_agent.py: Accept and propagate
  pipeline_id/file_urls in run_stream session state
- solune/frontend/src/components/chat/ChatInterface.tsx: Auto-fallback
  to project's assigned pipeline via useSelectedPipeline hook
- solune/backend/tests/unit/test_api_workflow.py: Updated pipeline
  override test to mock load_pipeline_as_agent_mappings
- solune/frontend/src/components/chat/ChatInterface.test.tsx: Added
  useSelectedPipeline mock

* review: normalize pipeline_id to empty string when falsy

- Applied `pipeline_id or ""` in both run() and run_stream() to prevent
  None from bypassing state.get("pipeline_id", "") default in agent_tools.
- Both methods now use the same normalization for consistency.

Evaluated against PR #1170 review recommendations.

refactor: restructure agent modules + documentation foundations (#34)

## Summary

Restructures the agent service for a clean foundation before adding the
agentic implementation feature.

### Phase 0 — Documentation + Instructions
- **Testing instruction**
(`.github/instructions/testing.instructions.md`) — modern testing best
practices (AAA, mock at boundaries, test behaviour not implementation)
- **Fixed stale instructions** — `python.instructions.md` had wrong
architecture description and file names; `docs.instructions.md` had
outdated requirement IDs (P1-P8 → P1-P12, R1-R20 → R1-R27)
- **ADR guidance** — added to copilot instructions and docs instructions
(when to write an ADR)
- **ADR-005: CI Access Pattern** — documents why Tailscale ephemeral +
scoped curl over SSH or Cloudflare
- **ADR-006: Dokploy GitOps** — documents auto-deploy with UI env vars,
env_file vs interpolation decision

### Phase 1 — Agent Code Refactoring
- **New `github.py`** — merges `github_app.py` auth with `review.py`'s
API helpers (fetch reviews, GraphQL, dismiss, stats). Single deep module
for all GitHub interaction.
- **Renamed `copilot_cli.py` → `copilot.py`** — simpler name
- **Renamed `worktree.py` → `git.py`** — clearer scope
- **Slimmed `review.py`** — pure orchestrator (~95 lines, was ~287).
Delegates all GitHub API calls to `github.py` and git ops to `git.py`.
- **Deleted `github_app.py`** — code merged into `github.py`
- **All 23 tests passing** (+2 new `bot_login` tests)

### Module structure (post-refactor)

| Module | Public interface | Hides |
|--------|-----------------|-------|
| `review.py` | `review_pr(repo, pr, model, effort)` | prompt building,
thread fetching, stale dismissal, stats |
| `copilot.py` | `run_copilot(cwd, prompt, model, effort, gh_token)` |
subprocess management, output parsing, stats extraction |
| `git.py` | `create_worktree()`, `cleanup_worktree()` | bare clone,
locks, ref management, stale cleanup |
| `github.py` | `get_token()`, `bot_login()`, `count_bot_reviews()`,
etc. | JWT, token caching, pagination, GraphQL, headers |

SM-10: Add MRO fast path before D2 fuzzy widening in resolveCallTarget (#741)

* Initial plan

* Add MRO fast path before D2 fuzzy widening in resolveCallTarget

When receiverTypeName is known, try resolveMethodByOwner (owner-scoped
+ MRO lookup) before falling back to the expensive lookupFuzzy in D2.
This short-circuits cross-file member call resolution for the common
non-overloaded case.

The fast path is skipped when overload disambiguation hints are
available (overloadHints or preComputedArgTypes) to avoid picking the
wrong overload for same-return-type overloaded methods.

Passes heritageMap to resolveCallTarget from all 4 call sites:
- Language seed path (processCalls)
- Sequential path (processCalls)
- walkMixedChain fallback
- Worker path (processCallsFromExtracted)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/sessions/9e49521f-2472-47bc-96e9-be4a46b073f0

Co-authored-by: magyargergo <11230420+magyargergo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(SM-10): address PR #741 review

Correctness:
- Module-alias guard for D0. When call.receiverName matches an active
  entry in ctx.moduleAliasMap for the current file, D0 is now skipped
  and resolution falls through to D1-D4 which respects the
  alias-narrowed candidate pool. Prevents a homonymous class in a
  different file from being picked by ctx.resolve(receiverTypeName)
  inside resolveMethodByOwner. New unit test pins the contract.

Unit tests (call-processor.test.ts — 3 new):
- D0 hit: child.parentMethod() resolves via MRO walk when
  heritageMap is provided.
- D0 skipped: same scenario still resolves via D1-D4 when heritageMap
  is undefined (backward-compat guard).
- Module-alias guard: two files both define class User with a save()
  method; 'import auth_mod as auth' in app.py must resolve
  auth.user.save() to auth_mod.py, not user_mod.py.

Integration language coverage (+3 fixtures/tests):
- swift-child-extends-parent — first-wins, gated on swiftAvailable.
- ruby-child-extends-parent   — first-wins.
- php-child-extends-parent    — first-wins (uses ParentClass since
  'Parent' is a PHP reserved word).

* test(SM-10): address second PR #741 review round

Unit tests (call-processor.test.ts, +2 new):
- overloadHints guard: Java source with two same-return-type overloads
  method(int) and method(String), int added first so lookupMethodByOwner
  would return it. processCalls auto-generates overloadHints for Java,
  forcing D0 to be skipped. o.method("hello") must resolve to
  method(String) via literal-inferred disambiguation.
- preComputedArgTypes guard: worker-path equivalent via
  processCallsFromExtracted with ExtractedCall.argTypes=['String'].
  Same two overloads, same correctness guarantee.

Integration tests (+2 fixtures + test blocks):
- go-child-extends-parent    — struct embedding, first-wins
  (Go structs are labeled 'Struct' not 'Class' in GitNexus).
- dart-child-extends-parent  — extends, first-wins, gated on
  dartAvailable like other Dart tests.

Documentation:
- Expanded the fallthrough comment in resolveMethodByOwner to clarify
  that unknown-extension paths land on plain lookupMethodByOwner
  without an ancestor walk, and that D1-D4 still runs on D0 miss.

* test(SM-10): D0 miss with heritageMap present falls through to D1-D4

Closes the last remaining gap from PR #741 review round 3. The existing
'D0 skipped' test only covered the heritageMap=undefined case, leaving
the miss-with-heritageMap path implicitly covered by integration tests
only. This adds a focused unit test where:

- Class Obj has a method doWork findable via tiered resolution
  (import-scoped) but intentionally NOT registered in methodByOwner
  (no ownerId), so lookupMethodByOwner misses.
- heritageMap is provided but built from an empty heritage array, so
  getAncestors(class:Obj) returns []. The MRO walk yields no parents.
- lookupMethodByOwnerWithMRO therefore returns undefined → D0 miss.
- D1 resolves the receiver type; D2 widens via lookupFuzzy;
  D3 file-filter picks the single matching candidate.
- A CALLS edge must still be emitted — D0 miss must not swallow
  the call.

connection. The gh-aw compiler now starts mcpg on the host; AWF only
launches the cli-proxy container and connects via TCP tunnel.

- --enable-cli-proxy

- TCP tunnel forwards localhost to host DIFC proxy for TLS

fix: stop hang on long recordings + force periodic analysis

minutes of audio) as one ONNX decode — extremely slow or hanging.

security(P1-4): add SecurityHeadersMiddleware with OWASP headers

Adds the following headers to every API response:

  X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
  X-Frame-Options: DENY
  X-XSS-Protection: 0  (defers to CSP on modern browsers)
  Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
  Permissions-Policy: disables camera/mic/geo/payment/USB
  Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'none'; frame-ancestors 'none'
  Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=63072000 (production only)

HSTS is gated to ENVIRONMENT=production to avoid breaking localhost
development over plain HTTP. CSP is maximally restrictive (none) because
this server only emits JSON and binary downloads, never HTML/scripts.

- 343 needs_review (7%) - genuine issues (compounds, high-freq UNK)

commit.message:
fix: escape pipe separators in _format_monster for MarkdownV2

The | character is a MarkdownV2 special character that must be escaped
as \|. In _format_monster, it was used raw in three places as a visual
separator (AC|HP line, CR|XP line, ability scores join), causing Telegram
to reject the message with a BadRequest. The fallback then stripped all
backslashes and disabled parse_mode, making raw asterisks/underscores
visible to the user.

Fix: replace all three | separators with \\| in the f-strings.

commit.message:
fix(framework): align template cursor in SSR hydration resolvers for nested blocks (#196)

When pathStart > 0 (used during in-place hydration of repeat and
conditional block items), $resolveSSR and $resolve skipped the
leading path segments but never advanced the template DOM cursor
through them.  This left the template cursor pointing at the wrapper
div from getTemplateDom() while the SSR cursor had already descended
to the block root element.

The misalignment caused path resolution to fail for any binding
nested deeper than one level inside a block:

- Issue #175 (variant selector): The inner for-loop over group.values
  inside the outer for-loop over optionGroups could not resolve its
  parent dd element.  The inner repeat was never hydrated, event
  handlers were never wired on the pill buttons, and clicking a
  variant after a direct page load (SSR) did nothing.

- Issue #176 (cart duplicates): The for-loop over cartItems inside
  the if-condition block could not resolve its parent ul element.
  An empty repeat was created with a new anchor in the wrong
  container.  When updateInstance ran syncRepeat, it created new DOM
  items alongside the SSR-rendered originals, doubling every cart
  line.

The fix adds a pre-advance loop in both resolveSSR (template-only)
and resolve (template path resolution) to walk the cursor through
path[0..pathStart-1] before the main resolution loop.  This is a
no-op for the common pathStart=0 case and adds exactly one extra
childNodes lookup for the pathStart=1 case used by block hydration.
Zero new allocations.

Regression tests added at two levels:

- Framework (webui-framework): 10 new SSR hydration tests for the
  nested-repeat fixture (light + shadow DOM), covering: no duplication
  after hydration, attribute preservation, reactive updates, list
  growth, and outer-scope text bindings.

- Commerce app (E2E): 3 new Playwright tests -- variant selector
  click activation after page refresh (#175), and cart line count
  after SSR reload (#176).

Closes #175, closes #176

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix tab resolution and AudioContext activation for slider

popup.js: getActiveTab() relied on lastFocusedWindow:true as the primary
query. When the popup window itself was the last focused window (e.g.
while the user interacted with the popup), Chrome returned the
popup page (chrome-extension:// URL) instead of the browser tab.
The code then resolved with that wrong result immediately without
falling back, so resolveTargetState() found a non-http URL, returned
null, and neither persistVolume nor sendVolumeToTab were ever called.

Fix: use currentWindow:true first (reliably refers to the host browser
window from a popup context), then fall back to lastFocusedWindow:true
with an isSupportedTabUrl guard, then finally walk all normal windows.

content.js: the VB_SET_VOLUME handler only called applyVolume() and
skipped ensureAudioContext(). On pages where the AudioContext was never
created (e.g. autoplay video with no prior user gesture), currentVolume
was set but applyGainAll() was a no-op because the context did not
exist. The contextEverRan guard also prevented a resume attempt.

Fix: call ensureAudioContext() + resumeAndApply() directly. The browser
may silently block the resume if its autoplay policy requires a gesture,
but the GainNode will be applied as soon as the context runs.

commit.message:
Fix Active Wars in World Analytics showing ? for kingdom names

War objects use kingdomA/kingdomB fields, but the analytics code
was looking for attackerId/defenderId, causing all war entries to
show '?' for names and 0 for military strength. Now checks both
field name patterns for compatibility.
